channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Oscar-winning actor Gene Hackman and his wife and dog found dead in their New Mexico home
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — Oscar-winning actor Gene Hackman, his wife and their dog were found dead in their New Mexico home Wednesday, authorities said. Foul play was not suspected, but authorities did not release circumstances of their deaths and said an investigation was ongoing.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Andrew Tate, who faces rape and trafficking charges in Romania, has left for the US
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — Influencer brothers Andrew and Tristan Tate, who are charged with human trafficking in Romania, have left for the U.S. after a travel ban on them was lifted, an official said Thursday. The brothers are also charged with forming a criminal gang to sexually exploit women.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: North Korea appears to have sent more troops to Russia to back its war against Ukraine, Seoul says
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s spy agency said Wednesday that North Korea appears to have sent additional troops to Russia, after its soldiers deployed on the Russian-Ukraine fronts suffered heavy casualties.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: As Trump's deadline to eliminate DEI nears, few schools openly rush to make changes
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Schools and colleges across the U.S. face a Friday deadline to end diversity programs or risk having their federal money pulled by the Trump administration, yet few are openly rushing to make changes.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, JOCELYN GECKER and CHEYANNE MUMPHREY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In lawsuit filing, Pentagon says transgender troops can't serve unless they meet a warfighting need
item.description: The Pentagon has revealed the specifics of its new transgender troop policy in a court filing that says any service member or recruit who has been diagnosed with or treated for gender dysphoria is disqualified from serving — unless they can prove they meet a specific warfighting need and adhere to s
item.dc:creator: Tara Copp, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Police killings set record in 2024, but with smallest increase in years: Report
item.description: In 2024, police officers in the US killed 1,365 people, a slight increase from the 1,329 civilians killed in 2023, according to the Mapping Police Violence report.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, WGN 9

item.title: Justice Department abandoning cases alleging discriminatory police and firefighter hiring
item.description: The Justice Department is abandoning Biden administration cases that sought to force police and fire departments to end alleged discriminatory hiring practices, Attorney General Pam Bondi said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Alanna Durkin Richer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: South Carolina man set to face a firing squad seeks postponement, questioning execution procedures
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — A South Carolina man who is scheduled to die next week by firing squad is again asking that his execution be postponed and prison officials release more information about the state's lethal injection drug and procedures.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: SEE IT: New York police save horse from icy pond in dramatic rescue
item.description: A horse that fell into an icy pond was rescued by police officers and neighbors after the animal struggled to get out of the cold water, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How AI, federal job cuts could impact Black workforce
item.description: In 1961, Martin Luther King Jr. warned of automation's impact on the Black labor force. Now, AI is sparking similar fears.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Texas transgender suspect plotted 'mass casualty attack' targeting police: FBI
item.description: A "mass casualty attack" was thwarted by Texas officials after learning a suspect allegedly made terroristic threats against police officers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bloody crime scene photos play key role in trial of man accused in Palestinian American boy's death
item.description: JOLIET, Ill. (AP) — Photos of a child's bloody body, a black knife holder and other crime scene evidence took center stage Wednesday at the murder and hate crime trial for an Illinois landlord accused of fatally stabbing a 6-year-old Palestinian American boy in 2023.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Immigration officials say everyone living in the US illegally must register: What to know
item.description: Federal immigration law has long required that people living illegally in the U.S. register with the government.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court hears arguments on straight woman's 'reverse discrimination' case
item.description: Marlean Ames filed suit against a state agency claiming she was not given a promotion because she is heterosexual.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mystery illnesses claim over 50 lives in Congo: What we know
item.description: Unidentified illnesses in northwestern Congo have killed more than 50 people over the past five weeks, nearly half of them within hours after they felt sick.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Judge criticizes Justice Department's broad reading of Trump's Capitol riot pardons
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Wednesday criticized the Justice Department's evolving position that a presidential pardon for a Kentucky man who stormed the Capitol also covers his conviction for illegally possessing guns at his home. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Kroger giving out free boxes for Daylight Savings Time
item.description: FARMERSBURG, Ind. (WTWO/WAWV)— The time change is less than two weeks away and Kroger is hoping to make that change a little easier. Starting on March 4 at noon, Kroger will be giving away what they refer to as the Hour Back Box.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Boyll, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Recent aviation disasters and close calls stoke fears about the safety of flying
item.description: All the recent aviation disasters and close calls have people worried about the safety of flying. The midair collision that killed 67 near Washington D.C. last month was the worst disaster.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Child becomes first measles death reported in Texas outbreak
item.description: An unvaccinated school-aged child who was hospitalized with measles has passed away, marking the first death in the outbreak, according to Lubbock and state health officials.
item.dc:creator: Caitlyn Rooney, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Computer simulations show nightmare Atlantic current shutdown less likely this century
item.description: The nightmare scenario of Atlantic Ocean currents collapsing, with weather running amok and putting Europe in a deep freeze, looks unlikely this century, a new study concludes.
item.dc:creator: BY SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NYC Mayor Eric Adams claims prosecutorial misconduct, asking judge to dismiss criminal case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams asked a federal judge to toss out the corruption case against him Wednesday, alleging prosecutorial misconduct, even as the Justice Department seeks dismissal of the charges on the Democrat's behalf.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Texas leads nation in housing ICE detainees
item.description: Immigration and Customs Enforcement detention facilities in Texas are holding the most immigrants, according to new data.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: iPhone bug that could suggest replacing 'racist' with 'Trump' is getting fixed
item.description: Apple is fixing a bug within the dictation feature on some iPhones that briefly suggests the word “Trump” when a word with an R consonant is spoken, including "racist.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Palm Beach, Florida hospital attack 'shouldn't have happened:' Nurse's daughter
item.description: "The fact that this happened at a healthcare facility is beyond me," Dr. Cindy Joseph said on "Banfield.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Things to know about the ruling blocking President Trump's refugee ban
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — President Donald Trump's effort to suspend the system for resettling refugees in the U.S. is on hold after a federal judge in Seattle blocked it. U.S.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Texas pipeline company’s $300M lawsuit against Greenpeace heads to trial in North Dakota
item.description: MANDAN, N.D. (AP) — A Texas pipeline company's lawsuit seeking potentially hundreds of millions of dollars from Greenpeace was set to advance with opening statements Wednesday in a trial the environmental organization calls an effort to silence critics of the oil industry.
item.dc:creator: JACK DURA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Scenes at US Capitol on war's anniversary reflect partisan divide on Ukraine
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — More than 100 House members at one point packed the steps of the U.S. Capitol to show their support for Ukraine following Russia's invasion three years ago, Republicans joining with Democrats in a forceful display of bipartisanship.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING and STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge indefinitely blocks Trump’s plan to freeze federal aid
item.description: A federal judge on Tuesday indefinitely blocked the Trump administration’s freeze of federal funding, dealing a stark blow to President Trump’s sweeping efforts to realign government spending with his agenda. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9

item.title: WATCH: Man caught hiding $10K of cocaine in toupée
item.description: A man was arrested in Columbia after trying to board a flight while wearing a wig hiding cocaine worth more than $10,000.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, WFLA, NBC

item.title: VA axes another 1,400 employees
item.description: The Department of Veterans Affairs on Monday fired another 1,400 employees amid outcry over a lack of transparency from the agency after 1,000 workers were axed earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, KRON4

item.title: Terror designation might prompt cartels to target Americans, expert says
item.description: The designation empowers the Treasury Department and U.S. law enforcement to aggressively target financial resources these groups utilize to produce their product, wage war on each other and authorities, and terrorize civilians in their home countries.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, Fox 5

item.title: FCC chairman climbs Nexstar broadcast tower to promote industry, jobs
item.description: The nation's top communications regulator made a stop in Baldwin County Monday morning and you might be surprised why.
item.dc:creator: Debbie Williams, KRON4

item.title: Supreme Court orders new trial for Oklahoma death row inmate
item.description: The Supreme Court on Tuesday sided with an Oklahoma death row inmate who claimed alongside the state itself that his trial was unfair, ordering a new trial and staving off his execution.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9

item.title: Live updates: House Republicans adopts budget after Trump whips GOP holdouts
item.description: House Republican leadership is working to pass a budget resolution on Tuesday to pave the way for President Trump's agenda, but opposition has emerged from all sides of the conference.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, Fox 5
